briefing.opening: Good morning. I will now begin the climate briefing for May 4, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Intensifying Climate Crisis Warnings and Current Challenges
sections.content: A leading climate change researcher has warned that this year will be the hottest on record [1], and internationally, criticism has been raised that despite the worsening climate change, there is a lack of further action [70]. Domestically, the increase in extreme heavy rainfall has exposed the limitations of existing flood volume calculation methods, which are slated for significant revision [19]. Internationally, climate change is driving extreme fluctuations in rivers, threatening fish habitats [72], and research has been published indicating that bee diversity in India's Odisha region is also threatened by habitat loss and climate change [74]. Notably, a report stating that methane emissions from Australian coal mines are more than double official estimates highlights the urgent need for robust greenhouse gas management [77].

sections.title: ⚠️ Setbacks Hindering Climate Action and the Shadow of Green Finance
sections.content: News has emerged that former U.S. President Donald Trump, who has expressed skepticism about climate change, has halted private wind power projects citing national security concerns, consequently stalling numerous renewable energy projects across the United States [16, 21, 45, 59]. This could pose a significant obstacle to global efforts in addressing the climate crisis. Furthermore, in the domestic green finance sector, concerns have been raised that foreign capital might be 'greenwashed' and erode climate budgets [7], highlighting the need for transparency in funding and oversight to ensure its intended purpose is met.

sections.title: 🌱 Practical Efforts for Climate Adaptation and Carbon Neutrality Domestically and Internationally
sections.content: As awareness of the climate crisis intensifies, various adaptation and carbon neutrality initiatives are continuing both domestically and internationally. In Gyeongnam province, farmers are actively cultivating high-income crops like mangoes, seizing climate change as an opportunity [5]. Gyeongsangnam-do Province plans to review and expand climate adaptation projects such as 'cool roofs,' 'cooling fog,' and 'cool roofs' in preparation for heatwaves [12, 20, 22]. Meanwhile, the Wonju Climate Change Promotion Center is operating experiential education programs for making flowerpots using coffee grounds [2, 3, 6, 13, 18, 25], and the Incheon Dong-gu Senior Welfare Center is conducting climate change education, demonstrating active efforts to promote citizen participation and awareness [4, 8, 11]. Gwangju City is establishing a virtuous cycle model by creating a 'Citizens' Forest' using carbon neutrality points donated by its citizens [24, 27, 31, 35, 37]. Chungcheongnam-do Province and Seongbuk-gu District are leading the way in achieving carbon neutrality in the building sector through green building policies and expanding mandatory green product procurement in the public sector, respectively [15, 23]. As such, diverse efforts for climate adaptation and carbon neutrality are continuing both domestically and internationally [71, 73, 75].

sections.title: 🌍 Accelerating Renewable Energy Transition and Market Changes
sections.content: Following the surge in oil prices due to the Middle East conflict, global interest and investment in renewable energy have dramatically increased [40, 41, 43, 54, 76]. Notably, over 4 trillion Korean Won flowed into renewable energy-related ETFs in the past month alone, and in the Jeju region, applications for private electric vehicle adoption have tripled, further highlighting the necessity of energy transition [41, 67]. China is strengthening its renewable energy leadership by operating eco-friendly energy projects directly connected to AI data centers, alongside its distributed renewable energy strategy [44, 53, 64]. Discussions at the International Maritime Organization (IMO) regarding the introduction of a marine carbon tax show potential for agreement within the year, making international transition movements more tangible [17, 65]. Domestically, Muan-gun and Hongcheon-gun are accepting applications for integrated renewable energy support projects [26, 48, 61], and Korea East-West Power is collaborating with its wind power subsidiaries to strengthen the renewable energy safety net [55, 56, 57, 60, 62], demonstrating active efforts to expand and stabilize renewable energy.

briefing.closing: In today's briefing, we examined the intensifying warnings of the climate crisis, actions that run counter to climate efforts, various climate adaptation and carbon neutrality initiatives being undertaken domestically and internationally, and finally, the accelerating renewable energy transition and market changes. The importance of global solidarity and tangible action to address the climate crisis is being emphasized now more than ever. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.title: [Summary News] How Far Has the 'Maritime Carbon Tax' Discussion Progressed? Climate Solutions: 'IMO, Agreement Possible Within the Year'
articles.original_title: [정리뉴스] ‘해양 탄소세’ 논의 어디까지 왔나…기후솔루션 “IMO, 연내 합의 가능성”
articles.description: Discussions on introducing a 'maritime carbon tax' in the international shipping sector are expected to reach a critical juncture this year. The International Maritime Organization (IMO) has re-examined the adoption of the 'Net-Zero Framework' (NZF) for shipping greenhouse gas emissions, increasing the possibility of an agreement by year-end.

summary.phenomenon: Discussions on introducing a 'carbon tax' in the international shipping sector are anticipated to reach a significant turning point this year. The UN's International Maritime Organization (IMO) has re-discussed the adoption of the 'Net-Zero Framework' (NZF), a greenhouse gas emission regulation for the shipping sector, as a key agenda item.
summary.cause: International efforts are underway to regulate greenhouse gas emissions in the shipping sector.
summary.outlook: It is assessed that the likelihood of IMO agreeing on the adoption of the Net-Zero Framework by year-end has increased.

articles.original_description: 평택항 항만 컨테이너 모습. 한수빈 기자국제 해운 분야의 ‘탄소세’ 도입 논의가 연내 중대 분수령을 맞을 것으로 전망된다. 유엔 산하 국제해사기구(IMO)가 해운 부문 온실가스 배출 규제인 ‘넷제로 프레임워크’(NZF) 채택 문제를 핵심 의제로 재논의하면서 연말 합의 가능성이 커졌다는 평가가 나온다.AP통신에 따르면 지난달 27일(현지시간)부터 닷새간 영국···

articles.original_title: 기아차, 28년만에 국내 판매 현대차 추월…쏘렌토 가장 많이 팔렸다
articles.description: Kia has surpassed Hyundai in domestic car sales for the first time since its incorporation into Hyundai Motor Group in 1998, with the Sorento being the top-selling model. This shift is attributed to increased sales of hybrid, electric, and recreational vehicles amid high oil prices.

summary.phenomenon: For the first time since its incorporation into Hyundai Motor Group in 1998, Kia surpassed Hyundai's domestic car sales last month (April 2026).
summary.cause: The increase in sales of hybrid electric vehicles (HEV), electric vehicles (EV), and recreational vehicles (RV) amid the era of high oil prices due to factors like the Middle East conflict is analyzed as the cause.

articles.title: 'Afraid of Fuel Prices... Let's Switch to Electric Vehicles' Jeju Sees Surge in EV Applications
articles.original_title: “기름값 무서워···전기차로 갈아타자” 제주 전기차 신청 폭주
articles.description: Jeju Island is experiencing a surge in applications for private electric vehicle subsidies, with numbers tripling year-on-year. This is attributed to soaring oil prices driven by the Middle East conflict, pushing the region close to its first-half target of 4,000 units and potentially exhausting the budget by May.

summary.phenomenon: Due to the ongoing surge in oil prices following the Middle East conflict, applications for private electric vehicle subsidies in Jeju have skyrocketed. As of the end of April, the number of applications reached 3,900, a threefold increase compared to the previous year.
summary.cause: The high oil price situation resulting from the Middle East conflict is a primary cause of increased demand for electric vehicle purchases.
summary.outlook: The region is nearing its first-half supply target of 4,000 units, and with continued growth in applications, the budget is projected to be exhausted by the end of May.

articles.original_description: 전년비 3배 늘어···상반기 목표 4000대 임박신청 증가세 지속···5월말 예산 소진 전망제주도청 주차장에 주차된 전기차. 박미라 기자중동 전쟁 여파로 유가가 고공행진을 이어가면서 제주지역 전기차 민간 보급 신청이 폭주하는 것으로 나타났다.제주도는 지난 4월말 기준 전기차 민간보급 사업 신청 건수가 3900대로 집계됐다고 4일 밝혔다. 당초 상반기 보급 ···

articles.title: 'Waste Plastic to New Plastic' Hyundai Department Store Finds a Solution
articles.original_title: “폐비닐 새 비닐로” 해법 찾은 현대백화점
articles.description: Amid soaring naphtha prices due to the Middle East conflict, Hyundai Department Store's 'Vinyl-to-Vinyl' waste plastic recycling initiative is proving effective. This eco-friendly campaign, which regenerates waste plastic into new bags, is establishing a self-sufficient resource model, having secured 200,000 plastic bags.

summary.phenomenon: As naphtha prices, a raw material for plastic, surged due to the Middle East conflict, Hyundai Department Store's 'Vinyl-to-Vinyl' waste plastic resource circulation process contributed to securing 200,000 plastic bags.
summary.cause: The sharp rise in naphtha prices due to the Middle East conflict caused difficulties in procuring plastic raw materials, leading to the 'Vinyl-to-Vinyl' waste plastic resource circulation process gaining attention as an alternative.
summary.outlook: The plastic regeneration activities, part of an ongoing eco-friendly campaign, are establishing themselves as a self-sufficient model for procuring resources during emergencies.

articles.original_description: 현대백화점 제공중동전쟁 여파로 비닐의 원료가 되는 나프타 가격이 급등하자 현대백화점의 폐비닐 자원순환 프로세스 ‘비닐 투 비닐’이 제 역할을 톡톡히 하고 있다. 친환경 캠페인 일환으로 이어온 비닐 재생산 활동이 비상 시 자원을 스스로 조달하는 자급형 모델로 자리잡고 있다.현대백화점은 폐비닐 자원순환 프로세스 ‘비닐 투 비닐’을 통해 비닐봉투 20만장을 확보···

articles.description: In today’s newsletter: As oil prices spike and geopolitical tensions rise​, a diverse group of governments is attempting to build momentum ​and rewrite the global energy scriptGood morning. The cost of fossil fuels is threatening to strangle the global economy once again. Last week, oil prices surged after the US president, Donald Trump, warned that a blockade of Iranian ports could last months – causing the price of oil to jump to its highest level since Russia’s invasion of Ukraine. With it, t

summary.phenomenon: Last week, oil prices surged, reaching their highest level since Russia's invasion of Ukraine. This occurred after the U.S. President warned that a blockade of Iranian ports could last for months.
summary.cause: Geopolitical tensions and the threat of an Iranian port blockade are the causes of the oil price surge, exacerbated by the global economy's reliance on fossil fuels.
summary.outlook: A diverse coalition of governments is attempting to lead the world towards a post-fossil fuel future and rewrite the global energy script.

articles.original_title: ‘Wake-up call’: methane emissions from Australian coalmines more than double official estimates, report finds
articles.description: International Energy Agency findings show government must commit to rapid cuts in emissions of greenhouse gas, climate experts saySign up for climate and environment editor Adam Morton’s free Clear Air newsletter hereEmissions of the potent greenhouse gas methane from Australian coalmines are more than double official government estimates reported to the UN, according to a new International Energy Agency report.Climate and energy analysts said the report had again highlighted an “enormous gap” i

summary.phenomenon: Emissions of methane, a potent greenhouse gas, from Australian coalmines are more than double the official government estimates reported to the UN, according to a new International Energy Agency (IEA) report.
summary.cause: Methane emissions from coalmines are the cause, but specific scientific reasons or background for the emissions being more than double official estimates were not mentioned in the article.
summary.outlook: Climate experts emphasize that governments must commit to rapid cuts in these greenhouse gas emissions, highlighting an 'enormous gap' in understanding and action.
